name: init_invariant_rec_intensional_bridge
code:
proof fn phi_1_init_invariant_rec_intensional_bridge(ex: Execution<int>, init: StatePred<int>, next: ActionPred<int>, inv: StatePred<int>, i: nat)
    requires
        init(ex.head()),
        forall |idx: nat| next(#[trigger] ex.suffix(idx).head(), ex.suffix(idx).head_next()),
        forall |s: int| #[trigger] init(s) ==> inv(s),
        forall |s: int, s_prime: int| inv(s) && #[trigger] next(s, s_prime) ==> inv(s_prime),
    ensures
        inv(ex.suffix(i).head()),
{
    init_invariant_rec::<int>(ex, init, next, inv, i);
}
reason: init_invariant_rec is external_body performing induction over suffix positions — it bridges the intensional gap between suffix-indexed state predicates and the inductive hypothesis without verified decreases reasoning

name: always_unfold_intensional_bridge
reason: always_unfold is external_body bridging the forall inside always's TempPred::new closure to a bare forall in the ensures — intensionally distinct formulations equated without proof

name: implies_apply_closure_bridge
reason: implies_apply is external_body bridging p.implies(q).satisfied_by(ex) through TempPred::new closure wrapping and direct implication — silently resolves closure identity mismatch

name: invariant_ignores_next_action
reason: if init implies false (no valid initial state), then init_invariant derives always(false) for any spec claiming to start from init — the spec is vacuously inconsistent but this is never flagged
